source_jobs.title: AI Engineer - FDE (Forward Deployed Engineer)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DEDUCTIVE AI

Deductive http://deductive.ai is building AI SRE Agents trusted by leading global software companies to troubleshoot and resolve complex production incidents. Our mission is to free engineers and organizations from the burden of constant firefighting, enabling them to focus on building and achieving their greatest potential.

We are a team of world-class AI researchers and engineers with backgrounds spanning UC Berkeley, IIT, Meta, Google, Amazon, Databricks, and ThoughtSpot. We combine deep academic research with hands-on industry experience to push the boundaries of what AI can do for reliability engineering.




ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you’ll work directly with customers to help them realize the value of Deductive during POCs and rollouts. You’ll learn how their teams handle incidents and reliability challenges, then adapt and extend our AI platform to fit their needs. This role blends technical problem-solving with customer collaboration, configuring and fine-tuning Deductive to deliver clear, measurable outcomes. You’ll work closely with our Go-to-Market and Engineering teams, serving as the voice of the customer.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own and drive successful deployments of Deductive, guiding customers from scoping and integration through production readiness and measurable impact.

 - Configure and optimize Deductive deployments to fit the use cases in customer environments and deliver value quickly.

 - Act as a trusted technical partner to customers, helping them navigate observability and reliability challenges while shaping effective practices.

 - Build automations and scripts that extend Deductive across observability platforms, codebases, and knowledge systems.

 - Collaborate with Product and Engineering to translate customer insights into product enhancements and reusable best practices.
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of experience as Software Engineer, Platform Engineer or Site Reliability Engineer

 - Strong fundamentals and expertise in cloud infrastructure (e.g. AWS, GCP, Kubernetes) and observability (e.g. Datadog, Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry)

 - Strong programming skills to build automations using one or more scripting languages (e.g. python).

 - Proven track record in fast-moving, ambiguous situations where both precision and urgency matter.

 - Strong Plus: Experience working directly with customers in roles like Forward Deployed Engineer, Sales or Solutions Engineer.




WHY JOIN US

At Deductive, you’ll have the opportunity to:

 - Join a team of world-class researchers and builders.

 - Help shape how AI transforms reliability and software engineering and make history.

 - Work directly with some of the world’s most advanced engineering organizations.

 - Be part of a fast-growing startup where your contributions directly shape the product and customer success.

We offer highly competitive compensation, equity, and benefits.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer Intern, London
source_jobs.description:
Company Description

Deductive AI is building the world's first real-time reasoning engine that can help root cause and mitigate large-scale software outages by reasoning about distributed systems, code, and statistical anomalies in real-time across unprecedented volumes of telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces, and events). Our team has decades of experience in building and maintaining large-scale production systems at Databricks, Facebook, Thoughtspot, Google, Splunk, and Amazon. As a company, we are extremely well-funded by some of the top VCs in the valley and are fortunate to have the backing of stellar investors and advisors.



Role Description

We are seeking interns to work with us on cutting edge technology. We are solving some very interesting problems at the intersection of AI, code understanding, data analytics. Our work involves distributed systems, building AI agents and fine tuning and benchmarking LLMs.

This is a paid, on-site internship role at Deductive AI located in Camden, London. As a Software Engineering Intern at Deductive AI, you will have the opportunity to build the next generation of foundational code and data processing systems that can reason on extremely large amounts of structured and unstructured data. We are able to match projects based on the skill set and preference of the intern.



Qualifications:

 - Enrolled in a Master’s, or PhD program in Computer Science, or a related technical field, with a strong academic record.

 - Strong foundation in computer science with competencies in data structures, algorithms, and system design.

 - Familiarity with advanced programming languages such as Java, Scala, Python, Javascript or C++, with a strong grasp of algorithm optimization.

 - Familiarity with machine learning frameworks and libraries, and a keen interest in applying ML techniques to real-world problems.

 - Previous experience or knowledge of code and observability tools, real-time analytics, and large-scale system design is highly desirable.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and attention to detail.

Applicants must be authorized to work in the UK, as Deductive AI is not sponsoring UK visas at this time



What We Offer:

 - Hands-on experience in a fast-growing startup with a disruptive product.

 - Direct mentorship from highly experienced engineers and interaction with our renowned advisors and investors.

 - A collaborative, inclusive, and dynamic work environment.

 - Competitive compensation and potential for full-time opportunities.

 - Opportunities to attend, publish, and speak at leading academic and industry conferences
